title: Inferring latches
body:
for incomplete if and case statements currently does not work. 
Testcase:

```
module top(                                                    
   (* PAD="1"*)                                                
   input wire c,                                               
   (* PAD="2"*)                                                
   input wire i,                                               
   (* PAD="3,4"*)                                              
   input wire [0:1]s,                                          
   (* PAD="0"*)                                                
   output reg q,                                               
   (* PAD="0"*)
   output reg r
    /* */);

/*
supposedly this should infer an async latch
It does not work.
generates a mux with 1 undriven input wire _s13
after synth q=c&i
the z seems to propagate thru the mux, aswell
(but maybe bug in tgt-atf)
*/
always @(c,i)
  begin
    if(c==1)
      q=i;
  end


always @(s,i)
  case(s[0:1])
  2'b10:
    r=i;
  endcase


endmodule

```

Here's a simple patch. http://members.aon.at/aklamme4/scratch/0001-Infer-Latches.patch
code_blocks:
module top(                                                    
   (* PAD="1"*)                                                
   input wire c,                                               
   (* PAD="2"*)                                                
   input wire i,                                               
   (* PAD="3,4"*)                                              
   input wire [0:1]s,                                          
   (* PAD="0"*)                                                
   output reg q,                                               
   (* PAD="0"*)
   output reg r
    /* */);

/*
supposedly this should infer an async latch
It does not work.
generates a mux with 1 undriven input wire _s13
after synth q=c&i
the z seems to propagate thru the mux, aswell
(but maybe bug in tgt-atf)
*/
always @(c,i)
  begin
    if(c==1)
      q=i;
  end


always @(s,i)
  case(s[0:1])
  2'b10:
    r=i;
  endcase


endmodule
